ex: Spain, Italy, Germany, Portugal, Belgium, Netherlands, Austria, Greece…
delai: 21 days minimum (after a first rabies vaccination)
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
note: Common EU rules (Regulation 576/2013): no rabies titer test and no quarantine when traveling between EU countries. Different conditions apply when arriving from outside the EU — check the EU entry rules for your country of origin.
sources: European Commission — Traveling with your pet in the EU

delai: 21 days + tapeworm treatment 1 to 5 days before
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
Echinococcus treatment given by a veterinarian 24 to 120 h (1 to 5 days) before arrival, recorded in the pet passport or health certificate.
note: Common EU rules (Regulation 576/2013): no rabies titer test and no quarantine when traveling between EU countries. The tapeworm treatment is mandatory for this destination.
sources: European Commission — Traveling with your pet in the EU

ex: EU/EEA destinations that require a tapeworm treatment.
delai: 21 days + tapeworm treatment 1 to 5 days before
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
Echinococcus treatment given by a veterinarian 24 to 120 h (1 to 5 days) before arrival, recorded in the pet passport or health certificate.
note: The 24–120 h tapeworm-treatment window is strict: outside that window, entry is refused.
sources: European Commission — Traveling with your pet in the EU

delai: 21 days + tapeworm treatment 1 to 5 days before
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
An EU pet passport is accepted for entry into Great Britain (otherwise an animal health certificate / AHC is required).
Given by a veterinarian 24 to 120 h before arrival.
note: Import rules are changing (a reform has been announced for 2026): check gov.uk.

delai: 21 days minimum (after a first rabies vaccination)
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
note: Conditions close to the EU's. Maximum 5 animals per person (non-commercial travel).
sources: FSVO (Switzerland) — Traveling with dogs and cats

delai: 21 days + tapeworm treatment 1 to 5 days before
requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
Echinococcus treatment given by a veterinarian 24 to 120 h (1 to 5 days) before arrival, recorded in the pet passport or health certificate.
note: Norway applies the EU rules and requires the tapeworm treatment.
sources: European Commission — Traveling with your pet in the EU

requis: ISO 11784/11785 identification, implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
First vaccination from 12 weeks of age, then a 21-day wait. Boosters up to date.
Issued by a veterinarian (records ID + vaccination). Travelers from outside the EU use an EU animal health certificate instead.
note: Conditions aligned with the EU / Switzerland.
sources: European Commission — Traveling with your pet in the EU

requis: Mandatory; Iceland has some of the strictest animal health conditions in Europe.
Rabies titer test and parasite treatments per the Icelandic protocol.
note: Strict, country-specific procedure: always confirm with the Icelandic authority (MAST) before planning the trip.
sources: MAST — Icelandic Food and Veterinary Authority

delai: A few weeks (certificates with strict timing)
requis: Issued by an official veterinarian within 24 h before boarding.
Veterinary exam at least 10 days before arrival.
note: To enter or re-enter the EU from Morocco, a rabies titer test (≥ 0.5 IU/ml, drawn at least 30 days after vaccination) is required: plan ahead if the EU is your next stop.

requis: Microchip ID and EU pet passport.
Dated between 30 days and 12 months before arrival.
Certificate of good health + official endorsement
Issued 48–72 h before departure, then endorsed by the official veterinary authority of your departure country.
note: Breeds classified as dangerous are banned. A rabies titer test may be required to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards.

ex: For dogs arriving from rabies-free or low-risk countries.
requis: On entry into the United States.
Online form; the receipt is valid 6 months and must be shown on arrival.
note: Since August 2024, the CDC has simplified entry for dogs arriving from rabies-free and low-risk countries (France, for example): no rabies certificate and no quarantine. Check your country of origin's status on the CDC list.
sources: CDC — Dogs from rabies-free or low-risk countries

requis: In French or English, listing the microchip number, vaccination date, expiration date, and vaccine brand.
Rabies vaccination mandatory for dogs over 3 months old.
ID number listed on the certificate.
note: No quarantine. The official CFIA AIRS tool is the authoritative reference.

requis: Inactivated vaccine; first vaccination at least 21 days before departure.
Issued by a veterinarian before departure.
Internal and external, recent, noted on the certificate.
note: Mexico does not require a rabies titer test (the blood test that measures rabies antibodies). Health inspection on arrival (SENASICA). Confirm with a Mexican consulate.
sources: SENASICA / Mexican consulate — verify before travel

requis: Inactivated vaccine for dogs over 3 months old, at least 21 days after the first vaccination.
Issued by a veterinarian and endorsed by the official veterinary authority before departure.
Internal and external, shortly before departure, noted on the certificate.
note: Confirm with VIGIAGRO (the Brazilian authority) and your consulate.

requis: ≥ 0.5 IU/ml, drawn at least 30 days after vaccination (EU-approved lab), then a 3-month wait.
note: Maximum 2 animals per traveler. Confirm with a Turkish consulate.

requis: Required for dogs arriving from France, among other countries — check your country of origin's status.
note: Some breeds are banned or restricted. The rabies titer test adds to the lead time.

requis: Rabies at least 21 days before departure + DHPPL (distemper, hepatitis, parvovirus, leptospirosis).
note: No rabies titer test and no quarantine if the permit paperwork is in order.
sources: Thai Embassy — Bringing Pets into Thailand

delai: Several months (permit + rabies titer test + quarantine)
requis: Mandatory; conditions depend on the rabies-risk category of your country of origin.
Blood drawn at least 28 days after vaccination (≥ 0.5 IU/ml).
Up to ~30 days at an approved facility, depending on category.
note: Quarantine length depends on the rabies-risk category AVS assigns to your country of origin: check with NParks/AVS.

requis: Implanted BEFORE the first rabies vaccination.
At least two inactivated or recombinant vaccines given after the microchip.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml; blood drawn on the day of the 2nd vaccination (or later).
Between the blood draw and arrival (otherwise quarantine on arrival).
Declaration to the authorities (AQS) + inspection at the airport.
note: The longest procedure of all: start 7 to 8 months before departure.

requis: Once all rabies conditions are met (about 6–7 months ahead).
Linked to the permit and documents.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml; blood drawn at least 180 days before entry (valid 12 months).
Minimum 10 days at the Mickleham facility (Melbourne).
note: Every step must be performed by a government-accredited veterinarian. Check your country of origin's group with the DAFF.

requis: Apply at least 6 weeks ahead (the category depends on your country of origin; France, for example, is category 3).
Minimum 10 days at an MPI-approved facility.
note: Long, strict procedure. Follow the official MPI guide.
sources: MPI (New Zealand) — Bringing cats and dogs to NZ

requis: ISO 11784/11785 standard, implanted before the rabies vaccination.
Up to date; last shot at least 30 days before entry.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml, drawn more than 30 days after vaccination (required for dogs from 'non-designated' countries — France, for example).
Official veterinary certificate endorsed by the veterinary authority of your departure country.
Up to 30 days if the rabies titer test is missing or the paperwork is incomplete.
note: For dogs from 'non-designated' countries (France, for example), a rabies titer test ≥ 0.5 IU/ml is needed to avoid the 30-day quarantine. One animal per person. Enter through a port with quarantine facilities.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel to China

requis: Form ANF 2M. Dog imports are "restricted": essentially limited to permanent relocation or a few specific cases.
No Objection Certificate from the Animal Quarantine and Certification Station.
Up to date (vaccine name, batch, date) if the animal is over 3 months old.
Official veterinary certificate (free of rabies, distemper, parvovirus, leptospirosis…).
note: Imports are VERY restricted — essentially limited to permanent relocation, with prior DGFT authorization. Some breeds deemed 'ferocious' are banned. No rabies titer test, but the authorization is the main hurdle.

requis: Issued by the Ministry of Agriculture before departure. Entry via approved points (e.g., Jakarta), NOT directly into Bali.
Given between 30 days and 12 months before departure.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml for an animal arriving from a country not free of rabies.
Official veterinary certificate from the country of origin.
About 14 days on arrival at authorized entry points.
note: IMPORTANT: Bali bans the direct import of dogs/cats, with no exceptions. Entry only through other points (Jakarta). Eligibility depends on your country of origin — check case by case.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel to Indonesia

requis: 15-digit ISO 11784/11785 chip, implanted before the rabies vaccination.
Dog over 3 months old, vaccinated between 30 days and 12 months before entry.
Official veterinary certificate issued within 7 days of entry and government-endorsed.
note: No permit needed for 2 or fewer animals traveling with their owner. No rabies titer test required. No quarantine if the paperwork is complete.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel to Vietnam

requis: Up to date, given after the microchip (extra steps apply to countries the APQA does not classify as rabies-free — France, for example).
≥ 0.5 IU/ml, approved lab; blood drawn 30 days to 24 months before boarding.
Official veterinary certificate (an EU pet passport can replace it).
Notice to APQA/QIA (mandatory for 5 or more animals).
note: No 3-month wait after a passing rabies titer test (unlike the EU/Japan): travel is possible as soon as the result is in. Non-compliant animals are quarantined at the owner's expense.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel to Korea

requis: License from Malaysia's DVS (e-permit), to be requested BEFORE departure.
Inactivated vaccine, at least 30 days before entry.
Malaysia Veterinary Certificate endorsed by the veterinary authority of the country of origin.
Distemper, parvovirus, hepatitis, leptospirosis up to date.
Minimum 7 days on arrival (extendable depending on risk).
note: ISO microchip strongly recommended. No rabies titer test required from many origin countries (France, for example). Some breeds deemed aggressive are banned.

requis: From the DAPH, at least 2 weeks before export; valid 90 days.
Given at least 30 days and at most 10 months before boarding.
Certificate of good health and vaccination issued by the veterinary authority of the country of origin.
Internal and external parasites, within 10 days of boarding.
30 days at home (agreement signed at Katunayake airport).
note: Inspection on arrival. Heartworm and Leishmania tests are often required. No rabies titer test.
sources: Sri Lanka Embassy — Importing Pet Animals

requis: Up to date, 30 days to 12 months before arrival (dogs 5 months and older).
Form VC-DC2 endorsed by the official veterinary authority of your departure country.
Obtain from the AFCD BEFORE travel (the group depends on your country of origin; France, for example, is Group II).
note: No quarantine for dogs from Group II countries (France, for example) if all conditions are met. Never book the flight before you have the permit. No rabies titer test for Group II.
sources: AFCD — Import of Dogs and Cats from Group II

requis: Inactivated vaccine; first vaccination 90 days to 1 year before departure.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml, WOAH-approved lab, 90 days to 1 year before departure (required for dogs from countries Taiwan does not classify as rabies-free — France, for example).
Official veterinary certificate from the country of origin.
Request from APHIA at least 20 days before shipment (free).
Mandatory after entry (≈ 21 days); a new blood sample is taken on site.
note: For dogs from countries Taiwan does not classify as rabies-free (France, for example): rabies titer test AND quarantine are mandatory. Allow time for the titer test (≥ 90 days) plus the quarantine.
sources: APHIA (BAPHIQ) — Importation of Dogs or Cats FAQ

requis: Microchip readable by an ISO scanner.
First vaccination at least 14 days before the permit application. Other vaccines: distemper, hepatitis, parvovirus, parainfluenza, leptospirosis.
International veterinary certificate issued within 10 days of departure.
Obtain online from the BAI before travel (valid 60 days).
Internal and external, 7 to 91 days before the SPSIC application.
note: The dog must be at least 120 days old when the SPSIC is requested. No rabies titer test and no quarantine. Pregnant females are not admitted.
sources: Bureau of Animal Industry (BAI) — Pet Import

requis: ISO microchip (or readable tattoo applied before July 2011).
Valid: more than 21 days and less than 12 months before departure.
Issued by your veterinarian and endorsed by an official government veterinarian before departure.
note: No rabies titer test to enter, BUT a rabies titer test is required to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards (Algeria is not on the EU's rabies-controlled list): have it done BEFORE departure.

requis: ISO 11784/11785 microchip implanted BEFORE the rabies vaccination.
Given between 30 days and 12 months before entry.
Veterinary certificate endorsed by the official authority shortly before departure.
Internal and external, given by a veterinarian before entry.
note: No rabies titer test required to enter. No quarantine if the paperwork is in order. A rabies titer test is required to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards: have it done before departure.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel US to Egypt

requis: ISO microchip implanted before or at the same time as the rabies vaccination.
Given between 30 days and 12 months before entry.
From the Director of Animal Health (valid 6 months).
Within 30 days: Brucella canis, Trypanosoma evansi, Babesia gibsoni, Dirofilaria immitis, Leishmania.
Internal and external, within 30 days of travel.
Completed within 10 days of travel, endorsed by the official veterinary authority.
note: No rabies titer test, but several blood tests. No quarantine if the paperwork is complete. A rabies titer test is required to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel US to South Africa

requis: More than 1 month and less than 12 months before departure.
Authorization issued by the Directorate of Veterinary Services (DSV).
International certificate issued less than 72 h before arrival, endorsed by the official veterinarian.
note: No rabies titer test to enter. A rabies titer test is mandatory to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards — have it done before departure.

requis: Up to date; rabies titer blood draw 4 weeks after the vaccination.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml, then a 3-month wait after the blood draw before travel.
Request from the Livestock and Veterinary Division at least 3 months before departure.
Negative results required (Ehrlichia within 45 days of departure).
Minimum 5 days in a quarantine facility on arrival.
note: Dogs must be neutered (except for breeding) and not pregnant. Some breeds are banned. Arrival by air freight only.
sources: Ministry of Agro-Industry (Mauritius) — Guidelines to bring dogs to Mauritius

requis: Between 21 days and 12 months before entry (dogs over 3 months old).
From the Directorate of Veterinary Services; valid 90 days.
Veterinary certificate issued by an accredited veterinarian in the country of origin.
note: No rabies titer test and no quarantine if the paperwork is in order. A rabies titer test is required to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards: have it done before departure.

requis: Recommended; declare it on the certificate.
Up to date and valid on the day of entry.
Endorsed by the veterinary authority, in Spanish, valid 60 days (an endorsed EU pet passport is accepted).
Internal and external, within 15 days before the certificate is issued.
Good health, within 10 days before the certificate is issued.
note: No permit and no quarantine for an accompanied dog. No rabies titer test required.
sources: SENASA — Ingresos con perros y/o gatos

requis: At least 1 month and at most 12 months before boarding.
Original from the veterinary authority of the country of origin, in Spanish, valid 10 days.
Broad-spectrum internal and external, within 30 days of boarding.
Within 10 days of departure; free of infectious and parasitic diseases.
note: The SAG process is free. Microchip recommended. No rabies titer test or quarantine for an accompanied dog.
sources: SAG — Ingreso de perros, gatos y hurones

requis: Mandatory; first vaccination at least 21 days before boarding.
Endorsed by the health authority of the country of origin, less than 10 days before entry.
Internal and external, within 60 days of boarding, noted on the certificate.
Issued at the ICA office at the airport on arrival.
note: Without a valid certificate, a 15-day home quarantine may apply. No rabies titer test on entry.
sources: ICA — Ingreso de perros y gatos a Colombia

requis: Mandatory from 3 months of age, up to date.
Original from the official authority of the country of origin (rabies + distemper, hepatitis, parvovirus, leptospirosis).
Internal and external, within 30 days of travel.
Health attested; SENASA inspection on arrival.
note: A prior import permit is required. No quarantine and no rabies titer test.
sources: SENASA Perú — Requisitos para el ingreso de mascotas

requis: Mandatory for dogs over 3 months old (distemper, hepatitis, parvovirus, leptospirosis, rabies).
From the health authority of the country of origin; vaccine dates and batch numbers must be valid on entry.
Internal and external, within 15 days of arrival.
note: For an accompanied traveler: no prior permit and no quarantine (the permit is only required for freight shipments). No rabies titer test.
sources: SENASA Costa Rica — Requisitos importación perros y gatos

requis: Required; vaccination schedule listed on the certificate.
From the competent authority of the country of origin, attesting to health and vaccination.
note: On arrival, the border veterinary service (IMV) issues a 'Release Certificate' after inspection. No rabies titer test or quarantine for a healthy accompanied dog.
sources: SENASA (Argentina) — Requisitos para viajar a Cuba con mascotas

requis: ISO 11784/11785 microchip (or tattoo), declared on the certificate.
Given between 1 month and 1 year before travel (vaccine name and batch number listed).
Signed by a veterinarian and countersigned by an official veterinarian of the country of departure.
Internal and external, noted on the certificate.
note: An endorsed official certificate (DIGEGA) is required. No rabies titer test or quarantine for a healthy pet dog.
sources: DIGEGA — Requisitos zoosanitarios para el ingreso de mascotas

requis: From 3 months of age and at least 30 days before travel (rabies, distemper, hepatitis, leptospirosis, parvovirus).
APOSTILLED or legalized at a Panamanian consulate, valid up to 30 days.
Issued by a veterinarian, valid up to 30 days.
Form to be sent 3 to 5 business days before arrival (MINSA).
At home; $130 fee per animal, paid at Tocumen airport.
note: Apostilled/legalized certificate and advance notification are mandatory; home quarantine applies. No rabies titer test.
sources: MIDA — Requisitos para la importación de perros y gatos

requis: Given after the microchip; dog over 3 months old; currently valid.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml, more than 30 days after vaccination; blood drawn 3 to 12 months before entry.
From the Department of Animal Resources; residents only, 1 animal per person per year.
Issued within 14 days of travel and endorsed by an official government veterinarian.
note: A rabies titer test is required even when arriving from the EU. The permit is tied to a residence permit. Confirm your file with the Ministry.

requis: At least 6 months of validity remaining at the time of travel.
Via the Anaam platform (MEWA), valid 30 days, usually through a resident sponsor.
Original, endorsed by the veterinary authority of the exporting country.
note: No rabies titer test required. Breeds classified as dangerous are banned (Pit Bull, Rottweiler, Mastiff…). Max 2 dogs per 6 months.

requis: Implant before the first rabies vaccination.
Dog at least 3 months old; given between 30 days and 1 year before arrival.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml at an approved lab, at least 30 days after the first vaccination.
At least 2 business days before boarding; written authorization required.
Israeli template endorsed by an official veterinarian, issued no more than 10 days before arrival.
note: Rabies titer test mandatory even when arriving from the EU. Dangerous breeds banned. Minimum age 4 months. No permit needed for up to 2 animals traveling with their owner.
sources: Gov.il — Import of dogs and cats

requis: Dog over 3 months old; between 21 days and 12 months before entry.
Official veterinary certificate; EU pet passport accepted as proof of vaccination.
Internal and external, in the days before entry.
note: No rabies titer test or quarantine for an accompanied animal (1–2 max). A rabies titer test is needed to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards.

requis: At least 21 days after the first vaccination.
The EU pet passport is sufficient for entry when arriving from the EU.
note: No rabies titer test, permit, or quarantine for non-commercial travel (5 animals max).
sources: Serbian Veterinary Directorate — Movement of pets

requis: Dog at least 12 weeks old; at least 21 days after the first vaccination.
Accepted for entry when arriving from the EU.
note: No rabies titer test, permit, or quarantine for non-commercial travel from the EU (5 animals max).
sources: European Commission — Bringing your pet to a non-EU country

requis: Dog over 12 weeks old; at least 21 days before entry.
Bilingual, endorsed by an official veterinarian, within 10 days of travel.
Internal and external, shortly before departure, recorded on the certificate.
note: No rabies titer test when arriving from the EU. A rabies titer test is needed to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel US to Albania

requis: Implant before or at the same time as the rabies vaccination.
Dog at least 12 weeks old; at least 21 days after the first vaccination.
≥ 0.5 IU/ml; blood drawn at least 30 days after vaccination and more than 3 months before entry.
Endorsed international veterinary certificate, valid 10 days.
note: Rabies titer test required on entry. No commercial flights: entry is by land. Confirm open border crossings given the ongoing war.

requis: Dog over 3 months old; between 21 days and 12 months before entry.
Veterinary certificate; EU pet passport accepted, valid 10 days.
Internal (including Echinococcus) and external, shortly before departure.
note: No rabies titer test to ENTER when arriving from the EU. WARNING: a rabies titer test is mandatory to enter or re-enter the EU afterwards — have it done before departure.
sources: USDA APHIS — Pet Travel US to Georgia
